# 基于 Spring AI 开发的 AI 项目目录结构

目前业界基于 Spring AI 的项目主要分为两种层次：**框架本身的模块结构** 和 **应用级项目的目录结构**。下面分别介绍。

---

## 一、应用级项目目录结构（最常见）

这是企业中基于 Spring AI 开发 RAG/Chatbot/Agent 应用时最典型的目录结构：

```
my-ai-app/
├── pom.xml (或 build.gradle)
├── src/
│   ├── main/
│   │   ├── java/com/yourcompany/aiapp/
│   │   │   ├── config/                    # 配置层
│   │   │   │   ├── AiConfig.java          # Spring AI 模型配置（ChatClient、EmbeddingModel）
│   │   │   │   ├── VectorStoreConfig.java # 向量数据库配置（PGVector/Milvus等）
│   │   │   │   ├── SecurityConfig.java    # 安全配置（JWT等）
│   │   │   │   └── RateLimitConfig.java   # 限流配置
│   │   │   │
│   │   │   ├── controller/                # 控制器层
│   │   │   │   ├── ChatController.java    # 聊天接口（支持SSE流式输出）
│   │   │   │   ├── DocumentController.java# 文档上传/管理接口
│   │   │   │   └── AgentController.java   # Agent 工作流接口
│   │   │   │
│   │   │   ├── service/                   # 业务逻辑层
│   │   │   │   ├── ChatService.java       # 聊天服务，调用 ChatClient
│   │   │   │   ├── RagService.java        # RAG 编排（检索+增强+生成）
│   │   │   │   ├── EmbeddingService.java  # 向量嵌入生成
│   │   │   │   ├── DocumentService.java   # 文档解析与处理
│   │   │   │   ├── IngestionService.java  # ETL 管道（分块、向量化、入库）
│   │   │   │   └── AgentService.java      # Agent/工具调用编排
│   │   │   │
│   │   │   ├── advisor/                   # Spring AI Advisor 层
│   │   │   │   ├── MemoryAdvisor.java     # 对话记忆增强
│   │   │   │   ├── RagAdvisor.java        # RAG 上下文增强
│   │   │   │   └── LoggingAdvisor.java    # 日志/可观测性
│   │   │   │
│   │   │   ├── tool/                      # Function Calling / 工具定义
│   │   │   │   ├── WeatherTool.java       # 天气查询工具
│   │   │   │   ├── DatabaseTool.java      # 数据库查询工具
│   │   │   │   └── SearchTool.java        # 搜索工具
│   │   │   │
│   │   │   ├── model/                     # 数据模型层
│   │   │   │   ├── dto/                   # 请求/响应 DTO
│   │   │   │   │   ├── ChatRequest.java
│   │   │   │   │   └── ChatResponse.java
│   │   │   │   └── entity/               # 数据库实体
│   │   │   │       ├── Conversation.java
│   │   │   │       └── Document.java
│   │   │   │
│   │   │   ├── repository/               # 数据访问层
│   │   │   │   ├── ConversationRepository.java
│   │   │   │   └── DocumentRepository.java
│   │   │   │
│   │   │   └── AiApplication.java        # 启动类
│   │   │
│   │   └── resources/
│   │       ├── application.yml            # 主配置（API Key、模型参数等）
│   │       ├── application-dev.yml        # 开发环境配置
│   │       ├── application-prod.yml       # 生产环境配置
│   │       ├── prompts/                   # Prompt 模板目录
│   │       │   ├── system-prompt.st       # 系统提示词模板
│   │       │   ├── rag-prompt.st          # RAG 提示词模板
│   │       │   └── agent-prompt.st        # Agent 提示词模板
│   │       └── documents/                 # 知识库文档（开发用）
│   │
│   └── test/
│       └── java/com/yourcompany/aiapp/
│           ├── service/
│           └── controller/
│
├── docker-compose.yml                     # PostgreSQL+pgvector / Redis 等
└── README.md
```

---

## 二、关键分层说明

| 层级 | 职责 | Spring AI 关键组件 |
|------|------|--------------------|
| **Config** | 配置 ChatClient、EmbeddingModel、VectorStore | `ChatClient.Builder`、`@ConfigurationProperties` |
| **Controller** | REST/SSE 接口，接收用户请求 | `Flux<String>` 流式响应 |
| **Service** | 核心业务逻辑，RAG 编排，Agent 调度 | `ChatClient`、`VectorStore`、`DocumentReader` |
| **Advisor** | 请求/响应的拦截与增强（类似 AOP） | `MessageChatMemoryAdvisor`、`QuestionAnswerAdvisor` |
| **Tool** | Function Calling 工具定义 | `@Tool` 注解、`ToolCallback` |
| **Model/DTO** | 数据传输对象 | — |
| **Repository** | 数据持久化 | Spring Data JPA |

---

## 三、Spring AI 框架本身的模块结构

如果你对框架源码级结构感兴趣，Spring AI 2.x 采用如下多模块设计：

```
spring-ai/
├── spring-ai-commons/              # 基础公共模块（JSON、可观测性）
├── spring-ai-model/                # AI 能力抽象（ChatModel、EmbeddingModel、ImageModel）
├── spring-ai-client-chat/          # 高级对话 API（ChatClient）
├── spring-ai-vector-store/         # 向量数据库抽象
├── spring-ai-rag/                  # RAG 框架
├── models/                         # 20+ 模型实现
│   ├── spring-ai-openai/
│   ├── spring-ai-anthropic/
│   ├── spring-ai-ollama/
│   ├── spring-ai-qwen/             # 通义千问
│   └── ...
├── vector-stores/                  # 20+ 向量存储实现
│   ├── spring-ai-pgvector-store/
│   ├── spring-ai-milvus-store/
│   ├── spring-ai-qdrant-store/
│   └── ...
├── document-readers/               # 文档读取器
│   ├── spring-ai-pdf-document-reader/
│   ├── spring-ai-tika-document-reader/
│   └── ...
├── memory/                         # 对话记忆存储
│   ├── spring-ai-memory-repository-jdbc/
│   └── spring-ai-memory-repository-mongodb/
├── spring-ai-spring-boot-autoconfigure/   # 自动配置
├── spring-ai-spring-boot-starters/        # Starter 依赖
└── spring-ai-bom/                         # 版本管理 BOM
```

---

## 四、当前业界技术栈搭配

| 组件 | 主流选择 |
|------|---------|
| **JDK** | 17+（推荐 21，支持虚拟线程） |
| **Spring Boot** | 3.4+（Spring AI 1.x）或 4.x（Spring AI 2.x） |
| **LLM 提供商** | OpenAI / Anthropic / Ollama（本地） / 通义千问 |
| **向量数据库** | PostgreSQL + pgvector（最主流，可与业务数据共库）或 Milvus |
| **对话记忆** | JDBC / Redis |
| **文档解析** | Tika（通用）、PDF Reader |
| **部署** | Docker Compose / Kubernetes |
| **可观测性** | Micrometer + Prometheus + Grafana |

---

## 五、核心设计理念

1. **抽象即自由** — 通过 `ChatClient` 和 `ChatModel` 屏蔽不同 LLM 厂商差异，一次编码可切换多个模型
2. **Advisor 模式** — 类似 Spring MVC 的拦截器，可以链式增强对话（加记忆、加 RAG 上下文、加日志）
3. **与 Spring 生态无缝集成** — 天然支持 Spring Security、Actuator、Spring Data、Spring Batch 等
4. **Prompt 模板化** — 将提示词放在 `resources/prompts/` 下用模板引擎管理，支持变量替换

这种结构已经是当前 Java/Spring 生态做 AI 应用的事实标准，适用于智能客服、文档问答、Agent 工作流等场景。

---

结合上面的目录结构和Spring ai官网，jdk使用21 Spring boot使用4.x的版本Spring ai版本根据Spring boot版本去匹配一个稳定正式版，生成一个项目级别的rules

```json
{
  "url": "https://docs.spring.io/spring-ai/reference/getting-started.html",
  "markdown": "Getting Started :: Spring AI Reference\n\n# Getting Started\n\nThis section offers jumping off points for how to get started using Spring AI.\n\nYou should follow the steps in each of the following sections according to your needs.\n\n| Spring AI supports Spring Boot 3.4.x and 3.5.x. |\n| --- |\n\n## Spring Initializr\n\nHead on over to [start.spring.io](https://start.spring.io/) and select the AI Models and Vector Stores that you want to use in your new applications.\n\n## Artifact Repositories\n\n### Releases - Use Maven Central\n\nSpring AI 1.0.0 and later versions are available in Maven Central. No additional repository configuration is required. Just make sure you have Maven Central enabled in your build file.\n\nMaven\n\nGradle\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n&lt;!-- Maven Central is included by default in Maven builds.\n     You usually don’t need to configure it explicitly,\n     but it's shown here for clarity. --&gt;\n&lt;repositories&gt;\n    &lt;repository&gt;\n        &lt;id&gt;central&lt;/id&gt;\n        &lt;url&gt;https://repo.maven.apache.org/maven2&lt;/url&gt;\n    &lt;/repository&gt;\n&lt;/repositories&gt;\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\nrepositories {\n    mavenCentral()\n}\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n### Snapshots - Add Snapshot Repositories\n\nTo use the latest development versions (e.g.&#96;1.1.0-SNAPSHOT&#96;) or older milestone versions before 1.0.0, you need to add the following snapshot repositories in your build file.\n\nAdd the following repository definitions to your Maven or Gradle build file:\n\nMaven\n\nGradle\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n&lt;repositories&gt;\n  &lt;repository&gt;\n    &lt;id&gt;spring-snapshots&lt;/id&gt;\n    &lt;name&gt;Spring Snapshots&lt;/name&gt;\n    &lt;url&gt;https://repo.spring.io/snapshot&lt;/url&gt;\n    &lt;releases&gt;\n      &lt;enabled&gt;false&lt;/enabled&gt;\n    &lt;/releases&gt;\n  &lt;/repository&gt;\n  &lt;repository&gt;\n    &lt;name&gt;Central Portal Snapshots&lt;/name&gt;\n    &lt;id&gt;central-portal-snapshots&lt;/id&gt;\n    &lt;url&gt;https://central.sonatype.com/repository/maven-snapshots/&lt;/url&gt;\n    &lt;releases&gt;\n      &lt;enabled&gt;false&lt;/enabled&gt;\n    &lt;/releases&gt;\n    &lt;snapshots&gt;\n      &lt;enabled&gt;true&lt;/enabled&gt;\n    &lt;/snapshots&gt;\n  &lt;/repository&gt;\n&lt;/repositories&gt;\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\nrepositories {\n  mavenCentral()\n  maven { url 'https://repo.spring.io/milestone' }\n  maven { url 'https://repo.spring.io/snapshot' }\n  maven {\n    name = 'Central Portal Snapshots'\n    url = 'https://central.sonatype.com/repository/maven-snapshots/'\n  }\n}\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nNOTE: When using Maven with Spring AI snapshots, pay attention to your Maven mirror configuration. If you have configured a mirror in your&#96;settings.xml&#96; like this:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n&lt;mirror&gt;\n    &lt;id&gt;my-mirror&lt;/id&gt;\n    &lt;mirrorOf&gt;*&lt;/mirrorOf&gt;\n    &lt;url&gt;https://my-company-repository.com/maven&lt;/url&gt;\n&lt;/mirror&gt;\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nThe wildcard&#96;*&#96; will redirect all repository requests to your mirror, preventing access to Spring snapshot repositories. To fix this, modify the&#96;mirrorOf&#96; configuration to exclude Spring repositories:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n&lt;mirror&gt;\n    &lt;id&gt;my-mirror&lt;/id&gt;\n    &lt;mirrorOf&gt;*,!spring-snapshots,!central-portal-snapshots&lt;/mirrorOf&gt;\n    &lt;url&gt;https://my-company-repository.com/maven&lt;/url&gt;\n&lt;/mirror&gt;\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nThis configuration allows Maven to access Spring snapshot repositories directly while still using your mirror for other dependencies.\n\n## Dependency Management\n\nThe Spring AI Bill of Materials (BOM) declares the recommended versions of all the dependencies used by a given release of Spring AI. This is a BOM-only version and it just contains dependency management and no plugin declarations or direct references to Spring or Spring Boot. You can use the Spring Boot parent POM, or use the BOM from Spring Boot (&#96;spring-boot-dependencies&#96;) to manage Spring Boot versions.\n\nAdd the BOM to your project:\n\nMaven\n\nGradle\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n&lt;dependencyManagement&gt;\n    &lt;dependencies&gt;\n        &lt;dependency&gt;\n            &lt;groupId&gt;org.springframework.ai&lt;/groupId&gt;\n            &lt;artifactId&gt;spring-ai-bom&lt;/artifactId&gt;\n            &lt;version&gt;1.0.0&lt;/version&gt;\n            &lt;type&gt;pom&lt;/type&gt;\n            &lt;scope&gt;import&lt;/scope&gt;\n        &lt;/dependency&gt;\n    &lt;/dependencies&gt;\n&lt;/dependencyManagement&gt;\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\ndependencies {\n  implementation platform(\"org.springframework.ai:spring-ai-bom:1.0.0\")\n  // Replace the following with the specific module dependencies (e.g., spring-ai-openai) or starter modules (e.g., spring-ai-starter-model-openai) that you wish to use\n  implementation 'org.springframework.ai:spring-ai-openai'\n}\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nGradle users can also use the Spring AI BOM by leveraging Gradle (5.0+) native support for declaring dependency constraints using a Maven BOM. This is implemented by adding a 'platform' dependency handler method to the dependencies section of your Gradle build script.\n\n## Add dependencies for specific components\n\nEach of the following sections in the documentation shows which dependencies you need to add to your project build system.\n\n[Chat Models](https://docs.spring.io/spring-ai/reference/api/chatmodel.html)\n\n[Embeddings Models](https://docs.spring.io/spring-ai/reference/api/embeddings.html)\n\n[Image Generation Models](https://docs.spring.io/spring-ai/reference/api/imageclient.html)\n\n[Transcription Models](https://docs.spring.io/spring-ai/reference/api/audio/transcriptions.html)\n\n[Text-To-Speech (TTS) Models](https://docs.spring.io/spring-ai/reference/api/audio/speech.html)\n\n[Vector Databases](https://docs.spring.io/spring-ai/reference/api/vectordbs.html)\n\n## Spring AI samples\n\nPlease refer to [this page](https://github.com/spring-ai-community/awesome-spring-ai) for more resources and samples related to Spring AI."
}
```

```json
{
  "url": "https://docs.spring.io/spring-ai/reference/api/chatmodel.html",
  "markdown": "Chat Model API :: Spring AI Reference\n\n# Chat Model API\n\nThe Chat Model API offers developers the ability to integrate AI-powered chat completion capabilities into their applications. It leverages pre-trained language models, such as GPT (Generative Pre-trained Transformer), to generate human-like responses to user inputs in natural language.\n\nThe API typically works by sending a prompt or partial conversation to the AI model, which then generates a completion or continuation of the conversation based on its training data and understanding of natural language patterns. The completed response is then returned to the application, which can present it to the user or use it for further processing.\n\nThe&#96;Spring AI Chat Model API&#96; is designed to be a simple and portable interface for interacting with various [AI Models](https://docs.spring.io/spring-ai/reference/concepts.html#_models), allowing developers to switch between different models with minimal code changes. This design aligns with Spring’s philosophy of modularity and interchangeability.\n\nAlso with the help of companion classes like&#96;Prompt&#96; for input encapsulation and&#96;ChatResponse&#96; for output handling, the Chat Model API unifies the communication with AI Models. It manages the complexity of request preparation and response parsing, offering a direct and simplified API interaction.\n\nYou can find more about available implementations in the Available Implementations section as well as detailed comparison in the [Chat Models Comparison](https://docs.spring.io/spring-ai/reference/api/chat/comparison.html) section.\n\n## API Overview\n\nThis section provides a guide to the Spring AI Chat Model API interface and associated classes.\n\n### ChatModel\n\nHere is the [ChatModel](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-ai/blob/main/spring-ai-model/src/main/java/org/springframework/ai/chat/model/ChatModel.java) interface definition:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\npublic interface ChatModel extends Model&lt;Prompt, ChatResponse&gt;, StreamingChatModel {\n\n\tdefault String call(String message) {...}\n\n    @Override\n\tChatResponse call(Prompt prompt);\n}\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nThe&#96;call()&#96; method with a&#96;String&#96; parameter simplifies initial use, avoiding the complexities of the more sophisticated&#96;Prompt&#96; and&#96;ChatResponse&#96; classes. In real-world applications, it is more common to use the&#96;call()&#96; method that takes a&#96;Prompt&#96; instance and returns a&#96;ChatResponse&#96;.\n\n### StreamingChatModel\n\nHere is the [StreamingChatModel](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-ai/blob/main/spring-ai-model/src/main/java/org/springframework/ai/chat/model/StreamingChatModel.java) interface definition:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\npublic interface StreamingChatModel extends StreamingModel&lt;Prompt, ChatResponse&gt; {\n\n    default Flux&lt;String&gt; stream(String message) {...}\n\n    @Override\n\tFlux&lt;ChatResponse&gt; stream(Prompt prompt);\n}\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nThe&#96;stream()&#96; method takes a&#96;String&#96; or&#96;Prompt&#96; parameter similar to&#96;ChatModel&#96; but it streams the responses using the reactive Flux API.\n\n### Prompt\n\nThe [Prompt](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-ai/blob/main/spring-ai-client-chat/src/main/java/org/springframework/ai/chat/prompt/Prompt.java) is a&#96;ModelRequest&#96; that encapsulates a list of [Message](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-ai/blob/main/spring-ai-model/src/main/java/org/springframework/ai/chat/messages/Message.java) objects and optional model request options. The following listing shows a truncated version of the&#96;Prompt&#96; class, excluding constructors and other utility methods:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\npublic class Prompt implements ModelRequest&lt;List&lt;Message&gt;&gt; {\n\n    private final List&lt;Message&gt; messages;\n\n    private ChatOptions modelOptions;\n\n\t@Override\n\tpublic ChatOptions getOptions() {...}\n\n\t@Override\n\tpublic List&lt;Message&gt; getInstructions() {...}\n\n    // constructors and utility methods omitted\n}\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n#### Message\n\nThe&#96;Message&#96; interface encapsulates a&#96;Prompt&#96; textual content, a collection of metadata attributes, and a categorization known as&#96;MessageType&#96;.\n\nThe interface is defined as follows:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\npublic interface Content {\n\n\tString getText();\n\n\tMap&lt;String, Object&gt; getMetadata();\n}\n\npublic interface Message extends Content {\n\n\tMessageType getMessageType();\n}\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nThe multimodal message types implement also the&#96;MediaContent&#96; interface providing a list of&#96;Media&#96; content objects.\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\npublic interface MediaContent extends Content {\n\n\tCollection&lt;Media&gt; getMedia();\n\n}\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nThe&#96;Message&#96; interface has various implementations that correspond to the categories of messages that an AI model can process:\n\nThe chat completion endpoint, distinguish between message categories based on conversational roles, effectively mapped by the&#96;MessageType&#96;.\n\nFor instance, OpenAI recognizes message categories for distinct conversational roles such as&#96;system&#96;,&#96;user&#96;,&#96;function&#96;, or&#96;assistant&#96;.\n\nWhile the term&#96;MessageType&#96; might imply a specific message format, in this context it effectively designates the role a message plays in the dialogue.\n\nFor AI models that do not use specific roles, the&#96;UserMessage&#96; implementation acts as a standard category, typically representing user-generated inquiries or instructions. To understand the practical application and the relationship between&#96;Prompt&#96; and&#96;Message&#96;, especially in the context of these roles or message categories, see the detailed explanations in the [Prompts](https://docs.spring.io/spring-ai/reference/api/prompt.html) section.\n\n#### Chat Options\n\nRepresents the options that can be passed to the AI model. The&#96;ChatOptions&#96; class is a subclass of&#96;ModelOptions&#96; and is used to define few portable options that can be passed to the AI model. The&#96;ChatOptions&#96; class is defined as follows:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\npublic interface ChatOptions extends ModelOptions {\n\n\tString getModel();\n\tFloat getFrequencyPenalty();\n\tInteger getMaxTokens();\n\tFloat getPresencePenalty();\n\tList&lt;String&gt; getStopSequences();\n\tFloat getTemperature();\n\tInteger getTopK();\n\tFloat getTopP();\n\tChatOptions copy();\n\n}\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nAdditionally, every model specific ChatModel/StreamingChatModel implementation can have its own options that can be passed to the AI model. For example, the OpenAI Chat Completion model has its own options like&#96;logitBias&#96;,&#96;seed&#96;, and&#96;user&#96;.\n\nThis is a powerful feature that allows developers to use model-specific options when starting the application and then override them at runtime using the&#96;Prompt&#96; request.\n\nSpring AI provides a sophisticated system for configuring and using Chat Models. It allows for default configurations to be set at start-up, while also providing the flexibility to override these settings on a per-request basis. This approach enables developers to easily work with different AI models and adjust parameters as needed, all within a consistent interface provided by the Spring AI framework.\n\nFollowing flow diagram illustrates how Spring AI handles the configuration and execution of Chat Models, combining start-up and runtime options:\n\nStart-up Configuration - The ChatModel/StreamingChatModel is initialized with \"Start-Up\" Chat Options. These options are set during the ChatModel initialization and are meant to provide default configurations.\n\nRuntime Configuration - For each request, the Prompt can contain a Runtime Chat Options: These can override the start-up options.\n\nOption Merging Process - The \"Merge Options\" step combines the start-up and runtime options. If runtime options are provided, they take precedence over the start-up options.\n\nInput Processing - The \"Convert Input\" step transforms the input instructions into native, model-specific formats.\n\nOutput Processing - The \"Convert Output\" step transforms the model’s response into a standardized&#96;ChatResponse&#96; format.\n\nThe separation of start-up and runtime options allows for both global configurations and request-specific adjustments.\n\n### ChatResponse\n\nThe structure of the&#96;ChatResponse&#96; class is as follows:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\npublic class ChatResponse implements ModelResponse&lt;Generation&gt; {\n\n    private final ChatResponseMetadata chatResponseMetadata;\n\tprivate final List&lt;Generation&gt; generations;\n\n\t@Override\n\tpublic ChatResponseMetadata getMetadata() {...}\n\n    @Override\n\tpublic List&lt;Generation&gt; getResults() {...}\n\n    // other methods omitted\n}\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nThe [ChatResponse](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-ai/blob/main/spring-ai-model/src/main/java/org/springframework/ai/chat/model/ChatResponse.java) class holds the AI Model’s output, with each&#96;Generation&#96; instance containing one of potentially multiple outputs resulting from a single prompt.\n\nThe&#96;ChatResponse&#96; class also carries a&#96;ChatResponseMetadata&#96; metadata about the AI Model’s response.\n\n### Generation\n\nFinally, the [Generation](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-ai/blob/main/spring-ai-model/src/main/java/org/springframework/ai/chat/model/Generation.java) class extends from the&#96;ModelResult&#96; to represent the model output (assistant message) and related metadata:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\npublic class Generation implements ModelResult&lt;AssistantMessage&gt; {\n\n\tprivate final AssistantMessage assistantMessage;\n\tprivate ChatGenerationMetadata chatGenerationMetadata;\n\n\t@Override\n\tpublic AssistantMessage getOutput() {...}\n\n\t@Override\n\tpublic ChatGenerationMetadata getMetadata() {...}\n\n    // other methods omitted\n}\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n## Available Implementations\n\nThis diagram illustrates the unified interfaces,&#96;ChatModel&#96; and&#96;StreamingChatModel&#96;, are used for interacting with various AI chat models from different providers, allowing easy integration and switching between different AI services while maintaining a consistent API for the client application.\n\n[OpenAI Chat Completion](https://docs.spring.io/spring-ai/reference/api/chat/openai-chat.html)(streaming, multi-modality &amp; function-calling support)\n\n[Microsoft Azure Open AI Chat Completion](https://docs.spring.io/spring-ai/reference/api/chat/azure-openai-chat.html)(streaming &amp; function-calling support)\n\n[Ollama Chat Completion](https://docs.spring.io/spring-ai/reference/api/chat/ollama-chat.html)(streaming, multi-modality &amp; function-calling support)\n\n[Hugging Face Chat Completion](https://docs.spring.io/spring-ai/reference/api/chat/huggingface.html)(no streaming support)\n\n[Google Vertex AI Gemini Chat Completion](https://docs.spring.io/spring-ai/reference/api/chat/vertexai-gemini-chat.html)(streaming, multi-modality &amp; function-calling support)\n\n[Amazon Bedrock](https://docs.spring.io/spring-ai/reference/api/bedrock.html)\n\n[Mistral AI Chat Completion](https://docs.spring.io/spring-ai/reference/api/chat/mistralai-chat.html)(streaming &amp; function-calling support)\n\n[Anthropic Chat Completion](https://docs.spring.io/spring-ai/reference/api/chat/anthropic-chat.html)(streaming &amp; function-calling support)\n\n| Find a detailed comparison of the available Chat Models in the [Chat Models Comparison](https://docs.spring.io/spring-ai/reference/api/chat/comparison.html) section. |\n| --- |\n\n## Chat Model API\n\nThe Spring AI Chat Model API is built on top of the Spring AI&#96;Generic Model API&#96; providing Chat specific abstractions and implementations. This allows an easy integration and switching between different AI services while maintaining a consistent API for the client application. The following class diagram illustrates the main classes and interfaces of the Spring AI Chat Model API."
}
```

已完成所有项目级别 Rules 的创建。以下是生成的 4 个规则文件概览：

---

### 规则文件清单

| 文件 | 作用域 | 说明 |
|------|--------|------|
| `spring-ai-project.mdc` | **始终生效** | 项目架构总览：技术栈版本、目录结构、分层架构、Maven BOM 配置 |
| `java-conventions.mdc` | `**/*.java` | Java 21 编码规范：Record/虚拟线程等新特性、命名规范、异常处理、依赖注入 |
| `spring-ai-patterns.mdc` | `**/*.java` | Spring AI 开发模式：ChatClient 用法、Advisor 链、Tool Calling、RAG 流程、流式响应 |
| `config-conventions.mdc` | `resources/**/*.{yml,yaml,properties,st}` | 配置文件规范：多环境管理、敏感信息注入、核心配置项模板、Prompt 模板管理 |

### 版本说明

- **Spring Boot**: `4.0.3`（当前最新稳定版）
- **Spring AI**: `2.0.0-M2`（当前与 Spring Boot 4.x 兼容的最新版本，GA 正式版预计 2026 年 5 月底发布，届时建议升级）
- **JDK**: `21`（已启用虚拟线程）

需要注意的是，Spring AI 2.0 目前仍处于 Milestone 阶段（M2），还不是正式 GA 版本。这是因为 Spring Boot 4.x 刚发布不久，Spring AI 团队正在适配中。如果你需要使用完全稳定的组合，可以考虑退回到 **Spring Boot 3.5.x + Spring AI 1.0.x**。我已经在规则中标注了这一点，等 GA 版本发布后只需更新 BOM 版本号即可。

_**Agent (model claude-4.6-opus-high-thinking, mode Agent)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
The user is asking two questions:

1. Is there any problem with setting all 4 rules to `alwaysApply: true`?
2. If keeping the original settings (not always apply), how do the non-always-apply rules work?

Let me explain how Cursor rules work with different settings. This is a knowledge question, so I'll answer directly.

In Cursor, rules have three modes:
- `alwaysApply: true` — Always included in every conversation context
- `alwaysApply: false` with `globs` — Automatically applied when files matching the glob pattern are open/being edited
- `alwaysApply: false` without `globs` — Manual, needs to be referenced by the user with @


The non-always-apply rules I created have `globs` set, so they'll automatically activate when you're working on matching files. This approach is better because it keeps your context window lean by only loading relevant rules, while still giving the AI focused guidance based on what you're actually editing. Setting everything to always apply would work, but it wastes context space on rules you might not need in every conversation.</details></think>

---

这是个很好的问题。两种方式都可以用，但有区别。让我解释一下。

## 全部设为 Always Apply 有没有问题？

**功能上没有问题**，完全可以这样做。但有一个代价：

- 每次你和 AI 对话时，**所有 4 个规则的内容都会被注入到上下文窗口中**，无论你当前在做什么
- 比如你只是在写 `README.md`，并没有编辑任何 Java 文件或 YAML 配置，Spring AI 的代码规范和配置规范也会占用上下文空间
- 对于目前只有 4 个规则来说影响很小，但随着项目规模增大、规则越来越多时，会浪费宝贵的上下文窗口

**结论：现阶段规则少，全部 Always Apply 完全没问题。**

---

## 我原来设置的方式是怎么工作的？

我设置了两种模式：

### 1. Always Apply（始终生效）
`spring-ai-project.mdc` — 每次对话都会自动加载，因为它是项目的总纲（技术栈、目录结构），无论你编辑什么文件都需要知道。

### 2. Globs 模式（按文件自动匹配）
其余 3 个规则设置了 `globs` 文件匹配模式：

| 规则 | globs | 何时自动生效 |
|------|-------|------------|
| `java-conventions.mdc` | `**/*.java` | 当你打开或编辑任何 `.java` 文件时 |
| `spring-ai-patterns.mdc` | `**/*.java` | 当你打开或编辑任何 `.java` 文件时 |
| `config-conventions.mdc` | `src/main/resources/**/*.{yml,yaml,properties,st}` | 当你打开或编辑配置文件时 |

**这些规则不需要你手动做任何事情。** 它们是全自动的 — Cursor 检测到你当前打开的文件匹配 `globs` 模式时，就会自动把对应规则注入到 AI 的上下文中。

举个例子：
- 你打开 `ChatService.java` 编辑 → Cursor 自动加载 `java-conventions.mdc` + `spring-ai-patterns.mdc`
- 你打开 `application.yml` 编辑 → Cursor 自动加载 `config-conventions.mdc`
- 你只是在聊天问问题，没打开文件 → 只有 `spring-ai-project.mdc` 生效

---

## 建议

如果你觉得规则数量不多、不想纠结这些细节，**全部设成 Always Apply 完全可以**，简单省心。

如果你想保持我原来的精细化设置（推荐），**不需要做任何额外操作**，Cursor 会根据你当前编辑的文件自动匹配加载，体验上是无感的。

[Chat Models 对比 \| Spring AI Alibaba](https://java2ai.com/en/integration/chatmodels/comparison)<br/><br/># Spring AI 阿里百炼 DashScope 通义千问集成概览<br/><br/>## 框架关系<br/>Spring AI Alibaba 是 Spring AI 的官方子项目，专门用于对接阿里巴巴的 AI 大模型服务（通义千问 Qwen 和百炼 Bailian）。Spring AI 提供统一的 API 标准，Spring AI Alibaba 则实现具体的适配。[1][2]<br/><br/>## 核心集成方案<br/><br/>### 通义千问集成（3 步）<br/>1. 添加依赖：`spring-ai-alibaba-starter-dashscope`<br/>2. 配置 API 密钥：`spring.ai.dashscope.api-key`<br/>3. 注入客户端：`DashScopeChatClient`[1]<br/><br/>### 百炼平台集成<br/>通过 `DashScopeAgent` 调用阿里云百炼大模型应用（支持智能体应用和工作流应用），需配置应用 ID 和 API Key。[2][4]<br/><br/>## OpenAI 兼容模式支持<br/>DashScope 服务平台提供 OpenAI API 兼容接口，可通过 `spring-ai-starter-model-openai` 依赖以标准 OpenAI 方式接入，配置 `base-url` 为 `https://dashscope.aliyuncs.com/compatible-mode`。[3]<br/><br/>## 性能对比（测试数据）[1]<br/>\| 指标 \| 通义千问（Qwen-Turbo） \| 百炼（Bailian-7B） \|<br/>\|------\|-------------------\|-----------------\|<br/>\| 响应时间 \| 380ms \| 520ms \|<br/>\| 中文准确率 \| 92.3% \| 89.7% \|<br/>\| 并发能力 \| 100 QPS \| 60 QPS \|<br/>\| RAG 召回率 \| 87.6% \| 91.2% \|<br/><br/>**适用场景**：通义千问适合代码生成，百炼适合专业领域（金融、法律）的知识库问答。[1]<br/><br/>## 最新状态<br/>Spring AI Alibaba 版本 1.0.0.2-1.0.0.3 已支持完整集成，支持多模型管理、流式输出、性能监控等企业级功能。[1][4] |

A GitHub discussion from December 2025 asking about support timelines received a response on January 5, 2026 stating "Working in progress."[1]<br/><br/>## Current Compatible Versions<br/><br/>The latest stable release is **version 1.1.2.0** (as of March 2025), which supports:[2][3]<br/>- **Spring AI**: 1.1.2<br/>- **Spring Boot**: 3.5.x<br/>- **Spring AI Extensions**: 1.1.2.1 or 1.1.2.0<br/><br/>Earlier versions (1.0.x series and 1.1.0.0) support Spring Boot 3.4.x with Spring AI 1.0.0 or 1.1.0 respectively.[3]<br/><br/>## Latest Release<br/><br/>The most recent version is **1.1.2.2** (March 10, 2025), featuring AgentScope integration and multiagent patterns.[4]<br/><br/>## Recommendation<br/><br/>For new projects, it's recommended to use the BOM (Bill of Materials) approach with version **1.1.2.0** to manage dependencies and avoid conflicts with Spring AI and Spring Boot.[3] |

</details>
</tool-use>

[Match OPENAI_BASE_URL with OpenAI · Issue #2415 · spring-projects/spring-ai](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-ai/issues/2415)<br/><br/># DashScope OpenAI Compatible Configuration with Spring AI (2025)<br/><br/>## Quick Setup<br/><br/>To use DashScope with Spring AI as an OpenAI-compatible endpoint, you have two approaches:<br/><br/>### Option 1: Using Official Spring AI OpenAI Starter (Simplest)<br/><br/>Add the dependency:[2]<br/>```xml<br/><dependency><br/>    <groupId>org.springframework.ai</groupId><br/>    <artifactId>spring-ai-openai-spring-boot-starter</artifactId><br/></dependency><br/>```<br/><br/>Configure `application.yml`:[2][4]<br/>```yaml<br/>spring:<br/>  ai:<br/>    openai:<br/>      base-url: https://dashscope.aliyuncs.com/compatible-mode<br/>      api-key: $\{DASHSCOPE_API_KEY\}<br/>      chat:<br/>        options:<br/>          model: qwen-max<br/>```<br/><br/>**Important:** Do not include `/v1` in the base-url, as Spring AI automatically appends the API path.[4]<br/><br/>### Option 2: Using Spring AI Alibaba Starter<br/><br/>Add the dependency:[1]<br/>```xml<br/><dependency><br/>    <groupId>com.alibaba.cloud.ai</groupId><br/>    <artifactId>spring-ai-alibaba-starter-dashscope</artifactId><br/></dependency><br/>```<br/><br/>Configure `application.yml`:[1]<br/>```yaml<br/>spring:<br/>  ai:<br/>    dashscope:<br/>      api-key: $\{AI_DASHSCOPE_API_KEY\}<br/>      base-url: https://dashscope.aliyuncs.com<br/>```<br/><br/>## Key Configuration Properties<br/><br/>For OpenAI-compatible mode:[2][3]<br/>- `spring.ai.openai.base-url` - DashScope compatible endpoint URL<br/>- `spring.ai.openai.api-key` - Your DashScope API key<br/>- `spring.ai.openai.chat.options.model` - Model name (e.g., qwen-max, qwen-turbo)<br/><br/>For Spring AI Alibaba DashScope:[1]<br/>- `spring.ai.dashscope.base-url` - Default: `https://dashscope.aliyuncs.com`<br/>- `spring.ai.dashscope.api-key` - Required API key<br/>- `spring.ai.dashscope.chat.completions-path` - Default: `/api/v1/services/aigc/text-generation/generation`<br/><br/>## Supported Features<br/><br/>Both approaches support:[4]<br/>- Synchronous chat<br/>- Streaming responses<br/>- Function calling (DashScope 2025-05+)<br/>- Text embeddings<br/>- Multimodal input (qwen-omni-turbo) |

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Now I have a clear picture:

1. Spring AI Alibaba does NOT support Spring Boot 4.x yet (still "working in progress")
2. But DashScope is OpenAI API compatible, so we can use the standard `spring-ai-starter-model-openai` with a custom `base-url` pointing to `https://dashscope.aliyuncs.com/compatible-mode`
3. This approach works with Spring AI 2.0.0-M2 + Spring Boot 4.0.3

However, I need to be careful. Spring Boot 4.0.3 + Spring AI 2.0.0-M2 is a milestone combination. Let me think about whether this will actually work...


The user explicitly specified these versions and asked for Spring Boot 4.x, so I should proceed with the OpenAI-compatible approach using DashScope, though there's a risk that Spring AI 2.0.0-M2 might have compatibility issues with Spring Boot 4.0.3. I'm outlining the project structure with the standard Spring Boot application layout, organizing the code into configuration, controllers, services, and data transfer objects.

Now I'm deciding on the API endpoints—I'll keep it straightforward with a streaming chat endpoint using Server-Sent Events since the user specifically asked for a simple streaming Q&A implementation.

For the dependencies, I need to configure the pom.xml with Spring Boot 4.0.3, Spring AI 2.0.0-M2, and the OpenAI starter for DashScope compatibility. I'm also considering whether webflux is necessary—Spring AI's streaming returns a Flux type, which technically requires the webflux dependency even when using SSE in a traditional MVC setup.

Actually, I can include both web and webflux starters since Spring Boot handles having both on the classpath, defaulting to MVC while still supporting Flux return types for streaming endpoints. The reactor-core dependency should come transitively through Spring AI's dependencies, so I'll rely on that rather than adding it explicitly. I'm going back and forth on the dependencies, but I'll settle on `spring-boot-starter-web` with Spring AI handling the reactive streaming—if that doesn't work out, I can add webflux. Now I'm setting up the project structure with the controller, service, request record, configuration file, and supporting classes.

I should also include Maven Wrapper for easier portability, and I need to verify whether Spring AI 2.0.0-M2 is available on Maven Central or if I need to add a milestone repository to the build configuration. The milestone repository is safer to include anyway, so I'll add it to the pom.xml configuration. Now I'm setting up the project structure with Spring Boot as the parent and configuring the necessary dependencies. Setting up the project dependencies...

I'm finishing the Maven configuration with the test dependency scope, build plugins for Spring Boot, and repository settings for milestone releases. Then I'm second-guessing whether Spring Boot 4.0.3 actually supports the web starter dependency I included.

Now I'm setting up the main application class with the standard Spring Boot entry point, and then building out the controller layer with a chat endpoint that handles streaming responses using reactive Flux. I'm setting up the ChatService with dependency injection, configuring it with a system prompt in Chinese that establishes the AI as a friendly assistant. The service provides two methods: a synchronous chat method that sends a message and returns the response content, and a streaming variant that returns a reactive Flux stream for real-time message delivery.

Now I'm adding the supporting pieces - a simple ChatRequest record to encapsulate the message payload, and the application configuration that points to Alibaba's DashScope API with the Qwen model, setting reasonable defaults for temperature and token limits.

For the project setup, I'll add a .gitignore file to exclude build artifacts and IDE files, and consider whether to include Maven wrapper scripts for easier project setup without requiring a pre-installed Maven installation.

I'm also double-checking the ChatClient API for Spring AI 2.0 to make sure the streaming approach with `.stream().content()` is still the right pattern, and it looks correct based on the documented usage. Before finalizing everything, I should decide whether to use both `spring-boot-starter-web` and `spring-boot-starter-webflux` together, or if one is sufficient for this use case. When both are on the classpath, Spring Boot uses Servlet. But `Flux` return types still work for SSE in Spring MVC controllers. The `spring-boot-starter-webflux` brings in reactor-core and the reactive web support.

Actually, looking at Spring AI's dependencies, `spring-ai-starter-model-openai` should already include reactor-core (since it supports streaming). So maybe I don't need webflux at all if I'm running a servlet-based app.

Let me just stick with `spring-boot-starter-web` and the Spring AI starter. Since Spring AI depends on reactor-core, `Flux<String>` will be available as a return type for SSE endpoints without needing the full webflux starter. This keeps things simple while still supporting streaming responses.</details></think>

项目已搭建完成并且编译通过。以下是项目的整体概览：

---

## 项目结构

```
my-ai-cursor/
├── pom.xml                                          # Maven 配置
├── .gitignore
├── src/main/java/com/my/ai/cursor/
│   ├── MyAiCursorApplication.java                   # 启动类
│   ├── config/                                      # 配置类（预留）
│   ├── controller/
│   │   └── ChatController.java                      # 聊天接口
│   ├── service/
│   │   └── ChatService.java                         # 对话核心逻辑
│   └── model/dto/
│       └── ChatRequest.java                         # 请求 DTO（Record）
├── src/main/resources/
│   ├── application.yml                              # 应用配置
│   └── prompts/
│       └── system-prompt.st                         # 系统提示词模板
└── src/test/java/com/my/ai/cursor/
    └── MyAiCursorApplicationTests.java              # 测试类
```

## 技术栈

| 组件 | 版本 |
|------|------|
| JDK | 21（虚拟线程已启用） |
| Spring Boot | 4.0.3 |
| Spring AI | 2.0.0-M2 |
| LLM 对接 | 阿里百炼 DashScope（OpenAI 兼容模式） |

## 提供的 API 接口

| 方法 | 路径 | 说明 |
|------|------|------|
| POST | `/api/chat` | 普通问答，阻塞式返回完整回答 |
| GET | `/api/chat/stream?message=你好` | 流式问答，SSE 逐 token 返回 |

## 启动前需要修改的内容

在 `src/main/resources/application.yml` 中修改以下两项：

```yaml
spring:
  ai:
    openai:
      api-key: sk-your-dashscope-api-key    # ← 替换为你的百炼 API Key
      chat:
        options:
          model: qwen-max                    # ← 替换为你要使用的模型名称
```

修改后执行 `mvn spring-boot:run` 即可启动项目，然后在浏览器中访问 `http://localhost:8080/api/chat/stream?message=你好` 即可体验流式问答。

---

_**User**_

现在调用@src/main/java/com/my/ai/cursor/controller/ChatController.java 的streamChat这个接口响应结果是乱码，如data:浣犲ソ锛佸緢楂樺叴瑙佸埌

data:浣狅紒

data:馃憢
data:
data:鎴戞槸涓€涓汉宸ユ櫤鑳�
给我解决一下

---

_**Agent (model claude-4.6-opus-high-thinking, mode Agent)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
The user is experiencing garbled/mojibake text in the SSE stream response. The text "浣犲ソ锛佸緢楂樺叴瑙佸埌" looks like UTF-8 encoded Chinese text being interpreted as a different encoding (likely ISO-8859-1 or GBK).

This is a classic encoding issue. The problem is that the SSE response doesn't explicitly specify UTF-8 charset. The `MediaType.TEXT_EVENT_STREAM_VALUE` is `text/event-stream` without charset specification, and the browser/client may default to a non-UTF-8 encoding.

The fix is to explicitly set the charset to UTF-8 in the `produces` attribute. Instead of `MediaType.TEXT_EVENT_STREAM_VALUE`, use `"text/event-stream;charset=UTF-8"`.


Now I'm looking at the controller code to apply this fix.</details></think>

**问题根因**：`MediaType.TEXT_EVENT_STREAM_VALUE` 的值是 `text/event-stream`，没有声明字符集，浏览器/客户端默认用了非 UTF-8 编码（如 ISO-8859-1）来解析中文，导致乱码。


好的，下面帮你详细分析。

---

## 先说结论：两个 starter 共存不会导致编码冲突

`spring-boot-starter-web` + `spring-boot-starter-webflux` 同时存在时，Spring Boot 检测到 Servlet 容器（Tomcat）会**自动选择 Servlet（MVC）模式**运行，WebFlux 只是作为一个库提供 `Flux`/`Mono` 等响应式类型。这不是乱码的根因。

---

## 真正的原因分析

乱码内容 `浣犲ソ锛佸緢楂樺叴瑙佸埌` 是典型的 **UTF-8 字节被当作 ISO-8859-1/Latin-1 解码** 的结果。问题出在 SSE 的响应链路中，`produces = "text/event-stream;charset=UTF-8"` 只解决了 HTTP 头的 Content-Type 声明，但实际还有几个环节可能出问题：

### 1. Tomcat 默认 Response 编码

Spring MVC 运行在 Tomcat 上，Tomcat 的 `HttpServletResponse` 默认编码可能不是 UTF-8。在 Spring Boot 4.x 中，虽然理论上已默认 UTF-8，但当 MVC 处理 `Flux<String>` 返回类型时，编码可能在序列化层被覆盖。

### 2. Spring MVC 处理 Flux 的内部机制

当 MVC 遇到 `Flux<String>` 返回值 + `text/event-stream` 时，会使用 `SseHttpMessageWriter` 或类似的处理器来写入响应。这个处理器在序列化 SSE `data:` 行时，可能用了平台默认编码而非 UTF-8。

### 3. `produces` 中 charset 可能没生效

在某些 Spring MVC 版本中，`produces` 里的 `charset` 部分会被忽略或被内部 `MediaType` 解析覆盖，特别是在 Servlet 模式下处理 reactive 返回类型这种"跨界"场景中。

---

## 解决方案建议

有几种方式可以尝试，按推荐优先级排列：

### 方案 A：去掉 `spring-boot-starter-web`，只用 `spring-boot-starter-webflux`

这是最干净的方案。让应用运行在纯 Netty + WebFlux 模式下，WebFlux 原生处理 `Flux<String>` + SSE，编码默认就是 UTF-8，不存在 Servlet 层的编码问题。

pom.xml 改动：去掉 `spring-boot-starter-web`，只保留 `spring-boot-starter-webflux`。代码无需任何修改，`@RestController`、`@GetMapping` 这些注解在 WebFlux 中同样生效。

### 方案 B：保留双 starter，强制全局 UTF-8 编码

在 `application.yml` 中增加：

```yaml
server:
  servlet:
    encoding:
      charset: UTF-8
      force: true
      force-response: true
```

`force-response: true` 会强制所有 Servlet 响应使用 UTF-8，不管 Content-Type 怎么协商。

### 方案 C：在 Controller 层手动设置 Response 编码

用 `HttpServletResponse` 显式设置编码：

```java
@GetMapping(value = "/stream", produces = "text/event-stream;charset=UTF-8")
public Flux<String> streamChat(@RequestParam String message, HttpServletResponse response) {
    response.setCharacterEncoding("UTF-8");
    return chatService.streamChat(message);
}
```

---

## 推荐

**方案 A** 最彻底 — 你这个项目目前没有用到任何 Servlet 专属功能（如 Filter、HttpSession 等），纯 WebFlux 模式完全够用，而且 SSE 流式输出本身就是 WebFlux 的强项。

如果以后需要加传统 MVC 功能，再换回双 starter + 方案 B 即可。

需要我帮你改的话，切换到 Agent 模式即可。

_**Agent (model claude-4.6-opus-high-thinking, mode Agent)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
The user is asking about the `server.servlet.encoding` configuration properties in Spring Boot - specifically `charset`, `force`, and `force-response`. Let me explain what each one does.</details></think>

---

这三个配置属于 Spring Boot 的 **Servlet 字符编码过滤器**（`CharacterEncodingFilter`）的控制项，用来解决 HTTP 请求和响应的编码问题。

---

## 逐个解释

### `charset: UTF-8`

指定字符编码为 UTF-8。这个值会被设置到 `CharacterEncodingFilter` 上，作为 Servlet 容器处理请求/响应时的默认编码。

- 影响 `request.getCharacterEncoding()`
- 影响 `response.getCharacterEncoding()`

### `force: true`

**强制覆盖请求（Request）的编码**。

- 默认是 `true`
- 当设为 `true` 时，即使客户端在请求头 `Content-Type` 中指定了其他编码（比如 `Content-Type: application/json; charset=GBK`），Spring 也会**无视它**，强制用 `charset` 指定的 UTF-8 来解析请求体
- 设为 `false` 时，只有在客户端没有指定编码的情况下才使用 UTF-8 作为默认值

### `force-response: true`

**强制覆盖响应（Response）的编码**。这是解决你乱码问题的关键。

- 默认是 `false`
- 当设为 `true` 时，不管框架内部或其他组件把 `response.setCharacterEncoding()` 设成了什么值，最终都会被**强制改回 UTF-8**
- 设为 `false` 时，只在响应没有显式设置编码的情况下才用 UTF-8

---

## 工作原理

这三个配置实际上控制的是 Spring Boot 自动注册的 `CharacterEncodingFilter`，它是 Servlet Filter 链中最早执行的过滤器之一：

```
客户端请求 → CharacterEncodingFilter → Controller → 响应输出
                  ↓
          force=true → 强制 request 编码为 UTF-8
          force-response=true → 强制 response 编码为 UTF-8
```

---

## 和你的乱码问题的关系

你的乱码场景是这样的：

1. `ChatService` 从阿里百炼拿到的数据是 **UTF-8 编码的中文**
2. 数据通过 `Flux<String>` 传到 SSE 响应
3. Tomcat 在写入 HTTP 响应体时，用了**非 UTF-8 编码**（可能是 ISO-8859-1）去序列化字符串
4. 浏览器收到的字节已经"损坏"了，所以显示乱码

加上 `force-response: true` 后，`CharacterEncodingFilter` 会在响应写出之前把编码锁定为 UTF-8，从而避免 Tomcat 内部用错误编码。

---

## 一句话总结

| 配置 | 作用对象 | 默认值 | 一句话 |
|------|---------|--------|--------|
| `charset` | 请求+响应 | UTF-8 | 指定用什么编码 |
| `force` | 请求 | true | 无视客户端声明的编码，强制用 charset |
| `force-response` | 响应 | **false** | 无视框架内部设置的编码，强制用 charset |

你的问题本质就是 `force-response` 默认为 `false`，导致响应编码没有被强制锁定为 UTF-8。

我当前处于 Ask 模式，无法直接编辑文件。但修复方式很简单：

## 原因

Spring Boot 4.0 把 `server.servlet.encoding.*` 这组配置**迁移到了** `spring.servlet.encoding.*` 下，旧路径已被标记为 Deprecated。

## 修复方式

把第 1-7 行从：

```yaml
server:
  port: 8080
  servlet:
    encoding:
      charset: UTF-8
      force: true
      force-response: true
```

改为：

```yaml
server:
  port: 8080

spring:
  servlet:
    encoding:
      charset: UTF-8
      force: true
      force-response: true
```

注意 `encoding` 那三行要移到已有的 `spring:` 节点下面，不要重复写两个 `spring:`。合并后的完整文件应该是：

```yaml
server:
  port: 8080

spring:
  application:
    name: my-ai-cursor

  servlet:
    encoding:
      charset: UTF-8
      force: true
      force-response: true

  threads:
    virtual:
      enabled: true

  ai:
    openai:
      base-url: https://dashscope.aliyuncs.com/compatible-mode
      api-key: sk-537fe04bb20444758dd760331a8e599b
      chat:
        options:
          model: qwen3.5-plus
          temperature: 0.7
          max-tokens: 2048
```

这是 Spring Boot 4.0 的迁移变更之一 — 把原来挂在 `server` 下的 Servlet 编码配置归到了 `spring` 命名空间下，使配置层级更统一。功能完全一样，只是路径变了。
